name: Invalid Packet - CRC Failure Case
description: Valid packet structure with intentionally corrupted CRC bytes for error detection testing
purpose: Verify CRC validation correctly rejects packets with corrupted CRC

crc_coverage.description: CRC would be calculated over Header through Payload (same as other vectors)
excludes: CRC field (2 bytes) - intentionally corrupted in this vector

crc_output.note: Correct CRC generated by firmware; transmitted value intentionally corrupted for error detection test
crc_output.corruption: Firmware CRC is 0x942E, but test will send 0xFFFF to trigger CRC validation failure

transmitted_packet.description: Complete packet as transmitted (with intentionally corrupted CRC)

expected_firmware_response.description: How firmware should respond to this packet
expected_firmware_response.expected_behavior: Firmware receives packet, calculates correct CRC, compares with transmitted 0xFFFF, sees mismatch, sends NAK with error code 0x04

verification.method: Negative test: firmware correctly rejects packet with corrupted CRC
verification.procedure: Executed firmware CRC16::Calculate([0x55, 0xAA, 0x05, 0x10, 0x00, 0x02, 0x00, 0x01], 8) = 0x5E1D (correct). Test sends 0xFFFF to trigger error detection.
verification.notes: Canonical error detection vector. Firmware rejects this packet due to CRC mismatch (0xFFFF != 0x5E1D).

testing_notes: Verifies CRC validation is working (critical security property)
Tests firmware error response to corrupted packets
Confirms daemon receives and handles NAK correctly
Ensures system can detect and report communication errors
This is a NEGATIVE test: we expect rejection

why_this_test_matters.rationale: CRC validation is the primary defense against data corruption
tests: Firmware CRC validation on received packets (detects corruption)
System reliability (can detect and report communication failures)

interoperability.requirement: Every implementation must handle CRC validation consistently
applies_to: Firmware reference implementation (must validate and reject)
Daemon (must send valid CRC, handle NAK responses)
interoperability.note: Error handling must be identical across all implementations
